details.message: stage final_review resolution changed between suspend and resume

details.body: In `list_project_ids` (src/adapters/fs.rs:658-695), the CorruptRecord check (`if !entry.path().join(PROJECT_CONFIG_FILE).is_file()`) is applied per-directory independently. If a project directory exists in the audit workspace without `project.toml` (which can happen because `mirror_project_file` silently ignores failures via `let _ =`, while `append_line` / `write_atomic` create the directory via `create_dir_all`), the function will error even if the live workspace has a valid project. The fix is to collect candidate names from both directories first, then validate using `FileSystem::project_root()` (which falls through to the valid location), or skip the CorruptRecord error for the audit directory and only enforce it for the canonical (live) location.

In `plan_shape_matches` (src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:695-731), the variable `_sidecar_matches_plan_json` is computed but never used — the underscore prefix suppresses the unused-variable warning. This means the sidecar integrity check (plan.shape.json vs plan.json hash consistency) is performed but its result is silently discarded. Either remove the dead computation entirely, or use the result (e.g., log a warning when the sidecar is stale, or use it as a signal to skip re-writing plan.shape.json). The current code misleads readers into thinking sidecar validation is happening.

In `copy_tree` (src/adapters/fs.rs:441-460), symlinks are followed implicitly. `entry.file_type()?.is_dir()` from `DirEntry` may not resolve symlinks on all platforms, but `fs::copy` always follows symlinks. A symlink in the audit workspace pointing outside the workspace will cause its target to be copied into the live workspace. Add a symlink check (`entry.file_type()?.is_symlink()` → skip or error) to prevent unexpected file expansion during migration, especially since the audit directory is git-tracked and could be manipulated.

Fix legacy `plan.json` compatibility in `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:403-404` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:503-527`, which feed `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:695-730`. When a persisted bundle was written before `explicit_id` existed, `bead_has_explicit_id()` falls back to `bead_id.is_some()`, so every canonical bead in the old file is treated as explicit. On any later rematerialization where only non-shape fields change, `plan_shape_matches()` compares the new plan against this miscomputed all-explicit shape, decides the shape changed, and clears lineage/progress. I reproduced this by stripping `explicit_id` from a persisted plan and rematerializing with only an `executive_summary` change; `completed_beads` dropped from 1 to 0.

Repair the new dual-root project storage logic in `src/adapters/fs.rs:432-438`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:535-538`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:658-715`, and `src/adapters/fs.rs:738-831`. `create_project_atomic()` writes new projects under `live_project_root()`, but `pending_delete_path()` is still rooted at `workspace_root_path()`, which stays on `.ralph-burning` until a live `workspace.toml` exists. `stage_delete()` therefore renames only the live copy while `project_root()` and `list_project_ids()` can still fall back to the mirrored audit copy, so the project never actually becomes invisible and delete/rollback leave divergent state behind. `cargo test project_store_stage_and_commit_delete_removes_project -- --nocapture` fails on this regression.

Fix 67 newly broken CLI integration tests. The dual-directory workspace split changed the application to read from `.git/ralph-burning-live/` (live path), but the test helper `project_root()` at tests/cli.rs:79 still hardcodes `.ralph-burning/projects` (audit path). Test fixtures write data to the audit path where the application no longer reads, causing silent data-not-found failures across 67 tests. Update `project_root()` and any test helpers that write fixture data to target the live workspace path (`FileSystem::live_project_root` or equivalent), and verify all 86 currently-failing CLI tests pass.

Remove dead code in `plan_shape_matches` at service.rs:713-717. The `if existing_plan_hash == current_plan_hash` branch (line 713-715) returns `Ok(existing_shape_signature == current_shape)`, which is identical to the fallthrough at line 717. The `current_plan_hash` parameter has no effect on the function's behavior. Either the guard was meant to enable an early-return optimization (skip recomputing the shape when hashes match) or the two branches were meant to return different results. Clarify the intended semantics and fix accordingly — if the branches should behave identically, remove the dead guard and the unused parameter.

Add a determinism test for `render_plan_json` output. The test `plan_md_is_deterministic` at bundle.rs:1217 verifies plan.md is byte-identical across repeated calls, but there is no equivalent test for plan.json. Add a test that calls `render_plan_json(&bundle)` twice on the same input and asserts byte equality, analogous to the existing plan.md test.

Address crash-safety gap in `ensure_live_workspace_seeded` at fs.rs:496-506. The function uses `workspace.toml` existence as a completeness sentinel, but `copy_tree` iterates `read_dir` in non-deterministic filesystem order. If `workspace.toml` is copied before all files and the process crashes mid-copy, subsequent calls see the sentinel and skip re-seeding, leaving the live workspace permanently incomplete. Consider writing the sentinel file last (after all other files are copied) or using a staging directory with atomic rename.

src/adapters/fs.rs:408-415 makes `workspace_root_path()` prefer `.git/ralph-burning-live` whenever `init` has created it, and milestone/requirements storage now follows that root (`src/adapters/fs.rs:2354-2358`, `src/adapters/fs.rs:4244-4245`). `initialize_workspace()` always creates the live root (`src/contexts/workspace_governance/mod.rs:35-57`), but there is no seeding or mirroring path for requirements runs or milestone artifacts. That breaks the existing on-disk contract: data written under `.ralph-burning/requirements` or `.ralph-burning/milestones` becomes invisible after init, and new plan artifacts stop landing in the audited tree. I reproduced this with `cargo test --test cli project_create_from_requirements_materializes_milestone_bundle_output`, which now fails with `requirements run not found`. Keep milestone/requirements data in `.ralph-burning`, or add the same live/audit seeding+mirroring logic that projects now use.

`infer_bead_explicit_id()` in `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:403-419` treats any fully-qualified bead id that matches the canonical implicit slot as `explicit_id = false`. That is not just a legacy shape: the planner stub itself emits qualified ids like `ms-stub.bead-1` (`src/adapters/stub_backend.rs:737-747`). Once misclassified, `progress_shape_signature()` includes title/workstream metadata for that bead (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:357-389`), and `persist_plan_locked()` will clear lineage/progress on a metadata-only rematerialization (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:622-636`). Qualified explicit ids need to stay explicit; otherwise an innocuous rename of an explicitly identified bead can wipe completion state. Add a regression test that rematerializes a bundle whose planner-supplied id is already `milestone.bead-1` and changes only descriptive fields.

In `src/cli/project.rs`, `resolve_bead_plan` breaks for legacy plan.json files written before this change. Legacy canonicalized plan.json has `bead_id: Some("milestone.bead-N")` but no `explicit_id` field (deserializes to `None`). The new `proposal_matches_bead_id` returns false because `None != Some(true)`. The new `proposal_is_title_fallback_candidate` also returns false because `(None.is_none() && Some(...).is_none())` is false AND `None == Some(false)` is false. Neither resolution path matches, silently degrading to `membership_confirmed: false` (losing per-bead flow_override and plan version validation). Fix: in `proposal_is_title_fallback_candidate`, treat `explicit_id: None` with `bead_id: Some(canonical_implicit_id)` as a title-fallback candidate — e.g. add a branch: `proposal.explicit_id.is_none() && proposal.bead_id.as_deref().is_some_and(|c| implicit_slot_matches(c, milestone_id, implicit_bead_id))`.

In `src/contexts/workflow_composition/drift.rs:237-260`, `sync_project_prompt_hash` writes the updated project.toml to `FileSystem::project_root()` (which resolves to the live workspace) but does not mirror the change to the audit workspace. Every other write path in this diff (`write_project_policy_config`, `write_run_snapshot`, `append_journal_line`, `write_payload_artifact`, `write_amendment`, `create_project`, prompt update/rollback) mirrors to the audit root. This omission means the audit `project.toml` will have a stale `prompt_hash` after drift sync. Add `FileSystem::mirror_project_file(base_dir, project_id, PROJECT_CONFIG_FILE, &updated);` after the `write_atomic` call.

Fix legacy explicit-bead plan consumption in `src/cli/project.rs:298-318`, `src/cli/project.rs:553-581`, and `src/cli/project.rs:821-851`. `project create-from-bead` now only treats a proposal as an ID match when `explicit_id == Some(true)`, but `load_milestone_bundle()` just deserializes stored `plan.json` and never backfills `explicit_id` for older plans. A legacy plan that contains an explicit `bead_id` but no `explicit_id` field therefore fails both the ID path and the title-fallback path, so the command silently drops `flow_override`, `plan_hash`, and `plan_version`. I reproduced this with a legacy `plan.json` containing `bead_id = "ms-alpha.bead-2"` and `flow_override = "docs_change"`: the created project came out as `quick_dev` with no plan metadata. Canonicalize or infer explicitness when loading `plan.json` (or relax the matcher to treat `explicit_id: None` + `bead_id: Some(_)` as explicit for backward compatibility).

Make plan-shape recovery compare against the last committed plan, not whichever `plan.json` is lying on disk, in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:219-223`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:621-667`, and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:696-717`. `persist_plan_locked()` writes `plan.json`/`plan.md`/`plan.shape.json` before it updates `status.json`; if a crash happens in that window, `snapshot.plan_hash` and task-run lineage still describe the old plan, but a later materialization recomputes `shape_matches` from the stray `plan.json` and may skip `clear_task_run_lineage()`. That preserves completed/failed counts and old lineage for a plan shape that was never committed, attaching stale progress to the new plan. The new `StoredPlanShape.plan_hash` field is currently dead data; use it (or `snapshot.plan_hash`) to verify that the shape you are comparing is for the same committed plan before deciding to preserve progress.

Treat legacy `plan.json` entries with a populated `bead_id` as explicit when backfilling `explicit_id`, instead of classifying `bead-1` / `ms-*.bead-1` as implicit-slot matches. In `src/cli/project.rs:586-605`, `backfill_legacy_explicit_bead_flags` leaves slot-matching IDs unset, and `src/cli/project.rs:846-862` then refuses ID-based matching unless `explicit_id == Some(true)`. Pre-upgrade plans never auto-filled implicit `bead_id`s, so an older planner-authored `bead_id: "bead-1"` or `"ms-alpha.bead-1"` is currently downgraded to title fallback only; once the live bead title drifts, `project create-from-bead` silently loses flow overrides and membership confirmation for a still-stable explicit bead. Add a regression test that loads a legacy plan with `bead_id: "bead-1"`, no `explicit_id`, and a renamed live bead title.

Reintroduce duplicate-title validation for implicit beads after canonicalization. `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:150-170` only rejects duplicate titles when `bead_id` is `None`, but `render_plan_json` now canonicalizes every implicit bead to have a `bead_id` plus `explicit_id: false` via `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:568-593`. That means a human-edited `plan.json` can contain two implicit beads with the same title and still pass `validate()`, even though `resolve_bead_plan` relies on title fallback for implicit beads and becomes ambiguous / errors in `src/cli/project.rs:827-842` and `src/cli/project.rs:864-879`. Validation should reject duplicate titles for all non-explicit beads, not just raw `bead_id == None` inputs.

In `src/cli/project.rs:586-595`, `backfill_legacy_explicit_bead_flags` now marks every legacy proposal with a populated `bead_id` as explicit. That regresses the already-supported compatibility path where a canonical `plan.json` is round-tripped through older tooling that preserves `bead_id` but drops the newer `explicit_id` field (`tests/cli.rs:2029-2044`). After such a round-trip, an originally implicit `ms-alpha.bead-2` is reclassified as explicit, so `resolve_bead_plan` matches it by id in `src/cli/project.rs:842-852` even after live title drift and incorrectly preserves `flow_override`, `plan_hash`, and `plan_version` for a bead whose identity was never explicitly pinned. The fix needs a backward-compatible discriminator that still treats missing-`explicit_id` canonical slot ids as implicit in this round-tripped case, plus a regression test for a stripped-`explicit_id` canonical plan with a renamed live bead.

Validate loaded milestone plans before using them in `project create-from-bead`. In `src/cli/project.rs:553-583`, `load_milestone_bundle()` deserializes `plan.json`, backfills legacy `explicit_id` flags, and returns the bundle without ever calling `MilestoneBundle::validate()`. That means unsupported `schema_version`s and semantic corruption in the authoritative plan artifact (for example duplicate bead IDs, broken acceptance coverage, or cross-milestone references) are silently accepted by downstream bead creation instead of failing fast, even though the bundle contract is versioned and `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:27-29` says consumers must check the schema version before processing. After backfilling legacy flags, validate the bundle and surface `CorruptRecord` if the plan is not a valid bundle.

Make plan-shape resets retry-safe by deferring lineage deletion until after the new plan/snapshot commit is durable. In `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:623-668`, a shape change calls `clear_task_run_lineage()` at `:636-638` before the later fallible writes (`write_plan_json`, `write_plan_md`, `write_plan_shape`, `write_snapshot`, and journal append) complete. If any of those later steps fail, the old `task-runs.ndjson` history is already gone while `status.json` and/or the old plan files may still describe the previous plan. That turns a transient write failure into permanent loss of bead progress/history. Stage the lineage reset atomically with the new plan/snapshot state, or at minimum move the destructive clear until after the new plan artifacts and snapshot have been written successfully.

In `canonicalize_bundle` (bundle.rs:574-637), acceptance_criteria values on beads are not trimmed or canonicalized, yet `validate()` (bundle.rs:218-236) compares them against acceptance_map IDs using `.trim()`. If a planner writes `acceptance_criteria: [" AC-1 "]` with whitespace, validation passes (trimmed comparison at line 223), but `derived_acceptance_coverage` (bundle.rs:513-537) uses the raw untrimmed string as a map key, which won't match the untrimmed `criterion.id`. This causes the cross-check at bundle.rs:290 (`declared != derived`) to produce a false-positive mismatch error. Fix: trim acceptance_criteria values during canonicalization, or reject whitespace in acceptance_criteria during validation (as is done for bead_id references).

`infer_bead_explicit_id` (bundle.rs) accepts `_milestone_id` and `_implicit_bead_id` parameters but ignores them both, returning `bead.explicit_id.unwrap_or(bead.bead_id.is_some())`. This means any bead with a `bead_id` set — even one matching its implicit slot (e.g., `bead_id: "bead-1"` for the first bead) — is classified as explicit=true during canonicalization. In contrast, `backfill_legacy_explicit_bead_flags` (project.rs:589-617) uses `implicit_slot_matches` to correctly classify slot-matching IDs as implicit. While the `explicit_id_hints` mechanism bridges this for shape comparison, the divergence means a bundle's first canonicalization (fresh) and a reload after stripping `explicit_id` (legacy path) produce different `explicit_id` values for slot-matching bead_ids. This affects duplicate-title validation (non-explicit beads are checked for title uniqueness) and could mask validation errors.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:643-669` introduces a non-recoverable inconsistency path. `persist_plan_locked` now commits `plan.json`/`plan.md`/`plan.shape.json`, updates `status.json`, and appends the plan journal event before calling `clear_task_run_lineage()`. If truncating `task-runs.ndjson` fails at that last step, the function returns an error after the new plan hash/version are already durable. On the next `materialize_bundle` retry, `should_refresh_plan` is false because `snapshot.plan_hash` and the plan artifacts already match (`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:436-447`), so `persist_plan_locked` is skipped and the stale lineage is never cleared. Any later snapshot reconciliation can then resurrect progress from beads that no longer exist in the new plan shape. The lineage reset needs to stay inside the same retryable transaction as the plan update, or the snapshot needs an explicit dirty flag that forces the reset to be retried.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:654-665` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:776-793` make pending lineage cleanup retryable only when `plan.shape.json` still parses and its stored `shape_signature` exactly matches the current binary’s recomputed shape. That is too brittle for a recovery marker: if the sidecar is missing/corrupt, or if `progress_shape_signature` changes between the failed write and the retry, `materialize_bundle()` will refresh `plan.json`/`plan.md` for the already-committed plan but silently skip `clear_task_run_lineage()`. The milestone is then left on the new plan/version with stale `task-runs.ndjson` from the old shape. The pending-clear state needs to be keyed off a stable committed identity (for example `plan_hash`/plan version) or stored somewhere more durable than the shape sidecar.

`src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:699-703` persists the new `lineage_reset_required` state only in `plan.shape.json`, but the execution paths in `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:843-849` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:984-1002`/`1051-1069` never consult that marker before reconciling from `task-runs.ndjson`. After a post-commit lineage-clear failure, callers can still invoke `record_bead_start`, `update_task_run`, or `repair_task_run`; those paths will immediately rehydrate snapshot counters from stale lineage rows that belong to the pre-rematerialization plan. The run mutation APIs should block or self-heal while a lineage reset is pending.

In src/cli/project.rs:803-809, 833-840, and 857-885, implicit proposals are still treated as authoritative matches via title fallback once `explicit_id` has been backfilled to `false`. Because src/cli/project.rs:313-319 stamps the current `plan_hash`/`plan_version` whenever `membership_confirmed` is true, a stale bead ID after an implicit reorder can still be bound to the new plan solely because the title matches. The same gap also turns legacy plans with duplicate implicit titles into false ambiguities, even though the current implicit slot ID is available during resolution. Amend `resolve_bead_plan` so implicit proposals are only confirmed when the bead ID also matches that proposal's current implicit slot; otherwise keep the fallback non-authoritative.

Extract shared implicit-slot-matching logic into a single function usable by both `bundle.rs` and `project.rs`. Currently `bead_matches_implicit_slot` (bundle.rs:429) and `implicit_slot_matches` (project.rs:899) implement identical logic with slightly different parameter types (`&str` vs `&MilestoneId`). If one is modified without the other, bead-resolution behavior silently diverges between canonicalization and runtime bead-plan resolution—a class of bug that is hard to catch since the two code paths are exercised by different test suites.

Tighten milestone-bundle validation around `explicit_id` so the rendered `plan.json` cannot carry contradictory bead identity metadata. Today `MilestoneBundle::validate` accepts `explicit_id=true` with no `bead_id`, and `explicit_id=false` with a non-slot custom `bead_id` (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:149-153`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:421-426`, `src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:592-606`). Downstream code then interprets those flags as authoritative: progress-shape generation strips title/workstream metadata for any bead treated as explicit (`src/contexts/milestone_record/bundle.rs:393-404`), while `resolve_bead_plan` only resolves by ID when `explicit_id == true` and only falls back to implicit-slot matching when `explicit_id == false` and the ID matches the slot (`src/cli/project.rs:863-896`). A malformed planner payload can therefore silently preserve lineage across an implicit reorder, or make a custom-ID bead impossible to re-associate during `project create-from-bead`. Reject those inconsistent combinations (or recompute `explicit_id` from `bead_id`/slot during canonicalization) and add regression tests for both cases.
